info.name: John
info.desc: The Holy Gospel According to John

1:
In the beginning the Word already existed.
The Word was with God,
and the Word was God.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
2: He existed in the beginning with God.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
3:
God created everything through him,
and nothing was created except through him.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
4:
The Word gave life to everything that was created,
and his life brought light to everyone.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
; 1:3-4 Or and nothing that was created was created except through him. The Word gave life to everything.
5:
The light shines in the darkness,
and the darkness can never extinguish it.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
; 1:5 Or and the darkness has not understood it.
6: God sent a man, John the Baptist,
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
1:6 Greek a man named John.
7: to tell about the light so that everyone might believe because of his testimony.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
8: John himself was not the light; he was simply a witness to tell about the light.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
9: The one who is the true light, who gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
10: He came into the very world he created, but the world didn’t recognize him.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
11: He came to his own people, and even they rejected him.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
12: But to all who believed him and accepted him, he gave the right to become children of God.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
13: They are reborn—not with a physical birth resulting from human passion or plan, but a birth that comes from God.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
14:
So the Word became human
 and made his home among us. He was full of unfailing love and faithfulness.
 And we have seen his glory, the glory of the Father’s one and only Son.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
1:14a Greek became flesh.; 1:14b Or grace and truth; also in 1:17.
15: John testified about him when he shouted to the crowds, “This is the one I was talking about when I said, ‘Someone is coming after me who is far greater than I am, for he existed long before me.’”
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
16: From his abundance we have all received one gracious blessing after another.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
1:16 Or received the grace of Christ rather than the grace of the law; Greek reads received grace upon grace.
17: For the law was given through Moses, but God’s unfailing love and faithfulness came through Jesus Christ.
Prologue: Christ, the Eternal Word
18:
No one has ever seen God. But the unique One, who is himself God,
 is near to the Father’s heart. He has revealed God to us.
The Testimony of John the Baptist
1:18 Some manuscripts read But the one and only Son.
19:
This was John’s testimony when the Jewish leaders sent priests and Temple assistants
 from Jerusalem to ask John, “Who are you?”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
1:19 Greek and Levites.
20: He came right out and said, “I am not the Messiah.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
21:
“Well then, who are you?” they asked. “Are you Elijah?”
“No,” he replied.
“Are you the Prophet we are expecting?”
“No.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
; 1:21 Greek Are you the Prophet? See Deut 18:15, 18; Mal 4:5-6.
22: “Then who are you? We need an answer for those who sent us. What do you have to say about yourself?”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
23:
John replied in the words of the prophet Isaiah:
“I am a voice shouting in the wilderness,
‘Clear the way for the 
Lord
’s coming!’”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
24: Then the Pharisees who had been sent
The Testimony of John the Baptist
25: asked him, “If you aren’t the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet, what right do you have to baptize?”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
26:
John told them, “I baptize with
 water, but right here in the crowd is someone you do not recognize.
The Testimony of John the Baptist
1:26 Or in; also in 1:31, 33.
27: Though his ministry follows mine, I’m not even worthy to be his slave and untie the straps of his sandal.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
28: This encounter took place in Bethany, an area east of the Jordan River, where John was baptizing.
Jesus, the Lamb of God
29: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
Jesus, the Lamb of God
30: He is the one I was talking about when I said, ‘A man is coming after me who is far greater than I am, for he existed long before me.’
Jesus, the Lamb of God
31: I did not recognize him as the Messiah, but I have been baptizing with water so that he might be revealed to Israel.”
Jesus, the Lamb of God
32: Then John testified, “I saw the Holy Spirit descending like a dove from heaven and resting upon him.
Jesus, the Lamb of God
33: I didn’t know he was the one, but when God sent me to baptize with water, he told me, ‘The one on whom you see the Spirit descend and rest is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.’
Jesus, the Lamb of God
34:
I saw this happen to Jesus, so I testify that he is the Chosen One of God.
”
The First Disciples
1:34 Some manuscripts read the Son of God.
35: The following day John was again standing with two of his disciples.
The First Disciples
36: As Jesus walked by, John looked at him and declared, “Look! There is the Lamb of God!”
The First Disciples
37: When John’s two disciples heard this, they followed Jesus.
The First Disciples
38: Jesus looked around and saw them following. “What do you want?” he asked them. They replied, “Rabbi” (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?”
The First Disciples
39: “Come and see,” he said. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon when they went with him to the place where he was staying, and they remained with him the rest of the day.
The First Disciples
40: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of these men who heard what John said and then followed Jesus.
The First Disciples
41:
Andrew went to find his brother, Simon, and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which means “Christ”
).
The First Disciples
1:41 Messiah (a Hebrew term) and Christ (a Greek term) both mean “anointed one.”
42: Then Andrew brought Simon to meet Jesus. Looking intently at Simon, Jesus said, “Your name is Simon, son of John—but you will be called Cephas” (which means “Peter” ).
The First Disciples
1:42 The names Cephas (from Aramaic) and Peter (from Greek) both mean “rock.”
43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Come, follow me.”
44: Philip was from Bethsaida, Andrew and Peter’s hometown.
45:
Philip went to look for Nathanael and told him, “We have found the very person Moses
 and the prophets wrote about! His name is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
46:
“Nazareth!” exclaimed Nathanael. “Can anything good come from Nazareth?”
“Come and see for yourself,” Philip replied.
47: As they approached, Jesus said, “Now here is a genuine son of Israel—a man of complete integrity.”
48: “How do you know about me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus replied, “I could see you under the fig tree before Philip found you.”
49: Then Nathanael exclaimed, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God—the King of Israel!”
50: Jesus asked him, “Do you believe this just because I told you I had seen you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than this.”
51: Then he said, “I tell you the truth, you will all see heaven open and the angels of God going up and down on the Son of Man, the one who is the stairway between heaven and earth. ”
1:51 Greek going up and down on the Son of Man; see Gen 28:10-17. “Son of Man” is a title Jesus used for himself.

1:
The next day
 there was a wedding celebration in the village of Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,
The Wedding at Cana
2:1 Greek On the third day; see 1:35, 43.
2: and Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
The Wedding at Cana
3: The wine supply ran out during the festivities, so Jesus’ mother told him, “They have no more wine.”
The Wedding at Cana
4: “Dear woman, that’s not our problem,” Jesus replied. “My time has not yet come.”
The Wedding at Cana
5: But his mother told the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
The Wedding at Cana
6: Standing nearby were six stone water jars, used for Jewish ceremonial washing. Each could hold twenty to thirty gallons.
The Wedding at Cana
2:6 Greek 2 or 3 measures [75 to 113 liters].
7: Jesus told the servants, “Fill the jars with water.” When the jars had been filled,
The Wedding at Cana
8: he said, “Now dip some out, and take it to the master of ceremonies.” So the servants followed his instructions.
The Wedding at Cana
9: When the master of ceremonies tasted the water that was now wine, not knowing where it had come from (though, of course, the servants knew), he called the bridegroom over.
The Wedding at Cana
10: “A host always serves the best wine first,” he said. “Then, when everyone has had a lot to drink, he brings out the less expensive wine. But you have kept the best until now!”
The Wedding at Cana
11: This miraculous sign at Cana in Galilee was the first time Jesus revealed his glory. And his disciples believed in him.
The Wedding at Cana
12: After the wedding he went to Capernaum for a few days with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples.
Jesus Clears the Temple
13: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover celebration, so Jesus went to Jerusalem.
Jesus Clears the Temple
14: In the Temple area he saw merchants selling cattle, sheep, and doves for sacrifices; he also saw dealers at tables exchanging foreign money.
Jesus Clears the Temple
15: Jesus made a whip from some ropes and chased them all out of the Temple. He drove out the sheep and cattle, scattered the money changers’ coins over the floor, and turned over their tables.
Jesus Clears the Temple
16: Then, going over to the people who sold doves, he told them, “Get these things out of here. Stop turning my Father’s house into a marketplace!”
Jesus Clears the Temple
17: Then his disciples remembered this prophecy from the Scriptures: “Passion for God’s house will consume me.”
Jesus Clears the Temple
2:17 Or “Concern for God’s house will be my undoing.” Ps 69:9.
18: But the Jewish leaders demanded, “What are you doing? If God gave you authority to do this, show us a miraculous sign to prove it.”
Jesus Clears the Temple
19: “All right,” Jesus replied. “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
Jesus Clears the Temple
20: “What!” they exclaimed. “It has taken forty-six years to build this Temple, and you can rebuild it in three days?”
Jesus Clears the Temple
21: But when Jesus said “this temple,” he meant his own body.
Jesus Clears the Temple
22: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered he had said this, and they believed both the Scriptures and what Jesus had said.
Jesus and Nicodemus
23: Because of the miraculous signs Jesus did in Jerusalem at the Passover celebration, many began to trust in him.
Jesus and Nicodemus
24: But Jesus didn’t trust them, because he knew all about people.
Jesus and Nicodemus
25: No one needed to tell him about human nature, for he knew what was in each person’s heart.
Jesus and Nicodemus

1: There was a man named Nicodemus, a Jewish religious leader who was a Pharisee.
2: After dark one evening, he came to speak with Jesus. “Rabbi,” he said, “we all know that God has sent you to teach us. Your miraculous signs are evidence that God is with you.”
3: Jesus replied, “I tell you the truth, unless you are born again, you cannot see the Kingdom of God.”
3:3 Or born from above; also in 3:7.
4: “What do you mean?” exclaimed Nicodemus. “How can an old man go back into his mother’s womb and be born again?”
5: Jesus replied, “I assure you, no one can enter the Kingdom of God without being born of water and the Spirit.
3:5 Or and spirit. The Greek word for Spirit can also be translated wind; see 3:8.
6: Humans can reproduce only human life, but the Holy Spirit gives birth to spiritual life.
3:6 Greek what is born of the Spirit is spirit.
7: So don’t be surprised when I say, ‘You must be born again.’
3:7 The Greek word for you is plural; also in 3:12.
8: The wind blows wherever it wants. Just as you can hear the wind but can’t tell where it comes from or where it is going, so you can’t explain how people are born of the Spirit.”
9: “How are these things possible?” Nicodemus asked.
10: Jesus replied, “You are a respected Jewish teacher, and yet you don’t understand these things?
11: I assure you, we tell you what we know and have seen, and yet you won’t believe our testimony.
12: But if you don’t believe me when I tell you about earthly things, how can you possibly believe if I tell you about heavenly things?
13: No one has ever gone to heaven and returned. But the Son of Man has come down from heaven.
3:13 Some manuscripts add who lives in heaven. “Son of Man” is a title Jesus used for himself.
14: And as Moses lifted up the bronze snake on a pole in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
15: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
3:15 Or everyone who believes will have eternal life in him.
16: “For this is how God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.
3:16  Or  For God loved the world so much that he gave.
17: God sent his Son into the world not to judge the world, but to save the world through him.
18: “There is no judgment against anyone who believes in him. But anyone who does not believe in him has already been judged for not believing in God’s one and only Son.
19: And the judgment is based on this fact: God’s light came into the world, but people loved the darkness more than the light, for their actions were evil.
20: All who do evil hate the light and refuse to go near it for fear their sins will be exposed.
21: But those who do what is right come to the light so others can see that they are doing what God wants. ”
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
3:21 Or can see God at work in what he is doing.
22: Then Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside. Jesus spent some time with them there, baptizing people.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
23: At this time John the Baptist was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there; and people kept coming to him for baptism.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
24: (This was before John was thrown into prison.)
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
25:
A debate broke out between John’s disciples and a certain Jew
 over ceremonial cleansing.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
3:25 Some manuscripts read some Jews.
26: So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Rabbi, the man you met on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you identified as the Messiah, is also baptizing people. And everybody is going to him instead of coming to us.”
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
27: John replied, “No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
28: You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
29: It is the bridegroom who marries the bride, and the bridegroom’s friend is simply glad to stand with him and hear his vows. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
30: He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
31: “He has come from above and is greater than anyone else. We are of the earth, and we speak of earthly things, but he has come from heaven and is greater than anyone else.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
3:31 Some manuscripts do not include and is greater than anyone else.
32: He testifies about what he has seen and heard, but how few believe what he tells them!
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
33: Anyone who accepts his testimony can affirm that God is true.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
34: For he is sent by God. He speaks God’s words, for God gives him the Spirit without limit.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
35: The Father loves his Son and has put everything into his hands.
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus
36: And anyone who believes in God’s Son has eternal life. Anyone who doesn’t obey the Son will never experience eternal life but remains under God’s angry judgment.”
John the Baptist Exalts Jesus

1:
Jesus
 knew the Pharisees had heard that he was baptizing and making more disciples than John
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
4:1 Some manuscripts read The Lord.
2: (though Jesus himself didn’t baptize them—his disciples did).
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
3: So he left Judea and returned to Galilee.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
4: He had to go through Samaria on the way.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
5: Eventually he came to the Samaritan village of Sychar, near the field that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
6: Jacob’s well was there; and Jesus, tired from the long walk, sat wearily beside the well about noontime.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
7: Soon a Samaritan woman came to draw water, and Jesus said to her, “Please give me a drink.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
8: He was alone at the time because his disciples had gone into the village to buy some food.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
9:
The woman was surprised, for Jews refuse to have anything to do with Samaritans.
 She said to Jesus, “You are a Jew, and I am a Samaritan woman. Why are you asking me for a drink?”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
4:9 Some manuscripts do not include this sentence.
10: Jesus replied, “If you only knew the gift God has for you and who you are speaking to, you would ask me, and I would give you living water.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
11: “But sir, you don’t have a rope or a bucket,” she said, “and this well is very deep. Where would you get this living water?
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
12: And besides, do you think you’re greater than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us this well? How can you offer better water than he and his sons and his animals enjoyed?”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
13: Jesus replied, “Anyone who drinks this water will soon become thirsty again.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
14: But those who drink the water I give will never be thirsty again. It becomes a fresh, bubbling spring within them, giving them eternal life.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
15: “Please, sir,” the woman said, “give me this water! Then I’ll never be thirsty again, and I won’t have to come here to get water.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
16: “Go and get your husband,” Jesus told her.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
17: “I don’t have a husband,” the woman replied. Jesus said, “You’re right! You don’t have a husband—
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
18: for you have had five husbands, and you aren’t even married to the man you’re living with now. You certainly spoke the truth!”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
19: “Sir,” the woman said, “you must be a prophet.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
20:
So tell me, why is it that you Jews insist that Jerusalem is the only place of worship, while we Samaritans claim it is here at Mount Gerizim,
 where our ancestors worshiped?”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
4:20 Greek on this mountain.
21: Jesus replied, “Believe me, dear woman, the time is coming when it will no longer matter whether you worship the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
22: You Samaritans know very little about the one you worship, while we Jews know all about him, for salvation comes through the Jews.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
23: But the time is coming—indeed it’s here now—when true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth. The Father is looking for those who will worship him that way.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
24: For God is Spirit, so those who worship him must worship in spirit and in truth.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
25: The woman said, “I know the Messiah is coming—the one who is called Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
26: Then Jesus told her, “ I Am the Messiah!”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
; 4:26 Or “The ‘I Am’ is here”; or “I am the Lord”; Greek reads “I am, the one speaking to you.” See Exod 3:14.
27: Just then his disciples came back. They were shocked to find him talking to a woman, but none of them had the nerve to ask, “What do you want with her?” or “Why are you talking to her?”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
28: The woman left her water jar beside the well and ran back to the village, telling everyone,
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
29: “Come and see a man who told me everything I ever did! Could he possibly be the Messiah?”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
30: So the people came streaming from the village to see him.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
31: Meanwhile, the disciples were urging Jesus, “Rabbi, eat something.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
32: But Jesus replied, “I have a kind of food you know nothing about.”
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
33: “Did someone bring him food while we were gone?” the disciples asked each other.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
34: Then Jesus explained: “My nourishment comes from doing the will of God, who sent me, and from finishing his work.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
35: You know the saying, ‘Four months between planting and harvest.’ But I say, wake up and look around. The fields are already ripe for harvest.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
4:35 Greek white.
36: The harvesters are paid good wages, and the fruit they harvest is people brought to eternal life. What joy awaits both the planter and the harvester alike!
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
37: You know the saying, ‘One plants and another harvests.’ And it’s true.
Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
38: I sent you to harvest where you didn’t plant; others had already done the work, and now you will get to gather the harvest.”
Many Samaritans Believe
39: Many Samaritans from the village believed in Jesus because the woman had said, “He told me everything I ever did!”
Many Samaritans Believe
40: When they came out to see him, they begged him to stay in their village. So he stayed for two days,
Many Samaritans Believe
41: long enough for many more to hear his message and believe.
Many Samaritans Believe
42: Then they said to the woman, “Now we believe, not just because of what you told us, but because we have heard him ourselves. Now we know that he is indeed the Savior of the world.”
Jesus Heals an Official’s Son
43: At the end of the two days, Jesus went on to Galilee.
44: He himself had said that a prophet is not honored in his own hometown.
45: Yet the Galileans welcomed him, for they had been in Jerusalem at the Passover celebration and had seen everything he did there.
46: As he traveled through Galilee, he came to Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was a government official in nearby Capernaum whose son was very sick.
47: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged Jesus to come to Capernaum to heal his son, who was about to die.
48: Jesus asked, “Will you never believe in me unless you see miraculous signs and wonders?”
49: The official pleaded, “Lord, please come now before my little boy dies.”
50: Then Jesus told him, “Go back home. Your son will live!” And the man believed what Jesus said and started home.
51: While the man was on his way, some of his servants met him with the news that his son was alive and well.
52: He asked them when the boy had begun to get better, and they replied, “Yesterday afternoon at one o’clock his fever suddenly disappeared!”
53: Then the father realized that that was the very time Jesus had told him, “Your son will live.” And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
54: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did in Galilee after coming from Judea.

1: Afterward Jesus returned to Jerusalem for one of the Jewish holy days.
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
2:
Inside the city, near the Sheep Gate, was the pool of Bethesda,
 with five covered porches.
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
5:2 Other manuscripts read Beth-zatha; still others read Bethsaida.
3: Crowds of sick people—blind, lame, or paralyzed—lay on the porches.
Jesus Heals a Lame Man

5:3 Some manuscripts add an expanded conclusion to verse 3 and all of verse 4: waiting for a certain movement of the water, 4for an angel of the Lord came from time to time and stirred up the water. And the first person to step in after the water was stirred was healed of whatever disease he had.
5: One of the men lying there had been sick for thirty-eight years.
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
6: When Jesus saw him and knew he had been ill for a long time, he asked him, “Would you like to get well?”
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
7: “I can’t, sir,” the sick man said, “for I have no one to put me into the pool when the water bubbles up. Someone else always gets there ahead of me.”
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
8: Jesus told him, “Stand up, pick up your mat, and walk!”
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
9: Instantly, the man was healed! He rolled up his sleeping mat and began walking! But this miracle happened on the Sabbath,
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
10: so the Jewish leaders objected. They said to the man who was cured, “You can’t work on the Sabbath! The law doesn’t allow you to carry that sleeping mat!”
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
11: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.’”
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
12: “Who said such a thing as that?” they demanded.
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
13: The man didn’t know, for Jesus had disappeared into the crowd.
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
14: But afterward Jesus found him in the Temple and told him, “Now you are well; so stop sinning, or something even worse may happen to you.”
Jesus Heals a Lame Man
15: Then the man went and told the Jewish leaders that it was Jesus who had healed him.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
16:
So the Jewish leaders began harassing
 Jesus for breaking the Sabbath rules.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
5:16 Or persecuting.
17: But Jesus replied, “My Father is always working, and so am I.”
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
18: So the Jewish leaders tried all the harder to find a way to kill him. For he not only broke the Sabbath, he called God his Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
19: So Jesus explained, “I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
20: For the Father loves the Son and shows him everything he is doing. In fact, the Father will show him how to do even greater works than healing this man. Then you will truly be astonished.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
21: For just as the Father gives life to those he raises from the dead, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
22: In addition, the Father judges no one. Instead, he has given the Son absolute authority to judge,
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
23: so that everyone will honor the Son, just as they honor the Father. Anyone who does not honor the Son is certainly not honoring the Father who sent him.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
24: “I tell you the truth, those who listen to my message and believe in God who sent me have eternal life. They will never be condemned for their sins, but they have already passed from death into life.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
25: “And I assure you that the time is coming, indeed it’s here now, when the dead will hear my voice—the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen will live.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
26: The Father has life in himself, and he has granted that same life-giving power to his Son.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
27: And he has given him authority to judge everyone because he is the Son of Man.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
5:27 “Son of Man” is a title Jesus used for himself.
28: Don’t be so surprised! Indeed, the time is coming when all the dead in their graves will hear the voice of God’s Son,
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
29: and they will rise again. Those who have done good will rise to experience eternal life, and those who have continued in evil will rise to experience judgment.
Jesus Claims to Be the Son of God
30: I can do nothing on my own. I judge as God tells me. Therefore, my judgment is just, because I carry out the will of the one who sent me, not my own will.
Witnesses to Jesus
31: “If I were to testify on my own behalf, my testimony would not be valid.
Witnesses to Jesus
32: But someone else is also testifying about me, and I assure you that everything he says about me is true.
Witnesses to Jesus
33: In fact, you sent investigators to listen to John the Baptist, and his testimony about me was true.
Witnesses to Jesus
34: Of course, I have no need of human witnesses, but I say these things so you might be saved.
Witnesses to Jesus
35: John was like a burning and shining lamp, and you were excited for a while about his message.
Witnesses to Jesus
36: But I have a greater witness than John—my teachings and my miracles. The Father gave me these works to accomplish, and they prove that he sent me.
Witnesses to Jesus
37: And the Father who sent me has testified about me himself. You have never heard his voice or seen him face to face,
Witnesses to Jesus
